triage.userImpact: Data replication degraded on production cluster
triage.impactDetail: One DataNode unreachable. Replication factor temporarily reduced. No data loss but write throughput down approximately 30%.

metadata.logTemplate: ERROR dfs.DataNode: Connection refused from {IP}. Timeout during block replication.
metadata.rawLogSample: 2026-04-18 10:16:05 ERROR dfs.DataNode: Connection refused from 192.168.1.10. Timeout during block replication.

hypotheses.title: DataNode 192.168.1.10 network partition
hypotheses.reasoning: Connection refused combined with heartbeat loss pattern is more consistent with network isolation than process crash.

hypotheses.title: NameNode metadata inconsistency after recent restart
hypotheses.reasoning: NameNode is still awaiting block reports from the missing DataNode; stale metadata state could be masking a recovered node.

hypotheses.title: Disk I/O saturation on replica target nodes
hypotheses.reasoning: Elevated write latency on remaining replicas could slow replication pipelines enough to surface as upstream timeouts.

evidence.text: Block replication timeout after 60s on pipeline targeting 192.168.1.10
evidence.sourceLogSnippet: 2026-04-18 10:16:05 ERROR dfs.DataNode: Connection refused from 192.168.1.10. Timeout during block replication.

evidence.text: DataNode 192.168.1.10 heartbeat loss sustained for 92 seconds
evidence.sourceLogSnippet: 2026-04-18 10:16:30 WARN namenode.BlockManager: no heartbeat from DN 192.168.1.10 for 92000ms

evidence.text: NameNode awaiting block reports from 1 of 5 DataNodes
evidence.sourceLogSnippet: 2026-04-18 10:17:02 INFO namenode.BlockManager: awaiting block reports from 1/5 DataNodes (missing: 192.168.1.10)

evidence.text: Disk write latency on healthy replicas rose to 180ms p95 (baseline 35ms)

actionPlan.action: Isolate DataNode 192.168.1.10 from cluster via dfsadmin decommission
actionPlan.rationale: Stops further replication attempts against the unreachable node and allows orderly re-replication to healthy DataNodes.

actionPlan.action: Check network connectivity from NameNode to 192.168.1.10 (ping, ssh, port 50010)
actionPlan.rationale: Distinguishes network partition from host failure before decommission is committed.

actionPlan.action: Verify replication factor on affected blocks via hdfs fsck
Run full fsck and schedule HDFS balancer after node recovery
actionPlan.rationale: Restore target replication factor and rebalance block distribution once the isolation incident is resolved.

executiveSummary.headline: HDFS DataNode network isolation delayed replication; no data loss observed
executiveSummary.impact: Write throughput down approximately 30% since 10:16 UTC. Replication factor temporarily reduced on blocks that had 192.168.1.10 in their pipeline. No customer-facing data loss.
executiveSummary.suspectedCause: DataNode 192.168.1.10 lost heartbeat and refused connections, most consistent with network partition isolating the node from the NameNode rather than a process crash on the node itself.
executiveSummary.mitigations: Isolate the node from the cluster, verify underlying network reachability, and trigger re-replication of affected blocks to restore target replication factor.
executiveSummary.prevention: Add heartbeat-loss alerting at a 30-second threshold, document the decommission runbook, and verify cluster-wide network monitoring covers all DataNode-to-NameNode paths.
